component.name: PaymentCard
component.business_context: Merchant dashboard widget displaying a saved payment method, allowing the merchant to select a default card or delete a saved card.

constraints: Card number must be displayed masked, showing only the last 4 digits, in the format '**** **** **** 1234'
Must display a card brand icon supporting at least Visa, Mastercard, and Amex

accessibility_gaps: No token specified for the 'selected' state indicator (tokens_referenced is empty); if implemented as a color-only change it fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color and needs a non-color cue plus aria-pressed/aria-selected/aria-checked

color.border.default (#D8DEE9) on color.surface.card/color.background.default (#FFFFFF) = ~1.3:1, FAILS the 3:1 minimum for UI component/non-text borders (WCAG 1.4.11) if used alone to convey the card's default or selected boundary
color.text.disabled (#94A3B8) on white = ~2.8:1, FAILS 4.5:1 for body text — must not be used for masked card number, expiry, or cardholder name in the default (non-disabled) state

If color.action.primary.default (#1A5CFF) is adopted as an ad-hoc 'selected' border on white it is ~4.0:1 (passes 3:1 for non-text/UI), but this is an unmapped/guessed token choice since no selection token exists in the design system reference for this component

No semantic role specified for the card's selectable behavior (e.g., role="radio"/aria-checked within role="radiogroup", or aria-selected within role="listbox"/role="group", or aria-pressed if a toggle button) — brief only says 'user can select the card'
Masked card number ('**** **** **** 1234') has no accessible-name strategy; screen readers may read masking glyphs literally instead of an announcement like 'Card ending in 1234'

Card brand icon (Visa/Mastercard/Amex) has no accessible-name requirement; per the Icon component's aria-hidden default it risks being unlabeled instead of exposing the brand as an accessible name or visually-hidden text

Delete action (icon-only) has no aria-label requirement (e.g., 'Delete card ending in 1234') and no confirmation step (role="alertdialog") despite being a destructive, hard-to-reverse action on sensitive payment data
No aria-live/role="status" specified to announce the result of select or delete actions to screen reader users
No visible focus ring specified for the selectable card region or the delete control (should use color.focus.ring)
No keyboard interaction model specified: Enter/Space to select the card, Tab order between card body and delete control, Escape to dismiss any delete-confirmation surface
No requirement that the delete icon-button remain independently focusable/operable and not nested as interactive-in-interactive with the card's own select action
No prefers-reduced-motion guidance for the selection-state transition (e.g., highlight/border animation) or for any card-removal animation on delete
No async/loading state defined for the delete action (e.g., Spinner while a delete request is in flight) or its announcement via role="status"
responsive_gaps: below breakpoint.sm: card stacks brand icon above masked number/name/expiry to avoid truncation
below breakpoint.md: action controls (select, delete) reflow below card details instead of inline trailing position
below breakpoint.lg: dashboard grid collapses from multi-column to single-column card list
above breakpoint.xl: card width is capped and centered within its grid cell rather than stretching full width
recommendations: Add a loading state that renders Skeleton placeholders for the masked number, expiry, cardholder name, and brand icon.
Add an empty state for when no saved payment card exists, with an affordance to add a new card and no error styling.
Add an error state (e.g. failed to load or delete) using Alert with role="alert", surfaced without discarding the card's last-known data if present.
Bind the delete action to a confirmation step so a stray click cannot silently remove a saved payment method.
Expose selection state via a prop-driven boolean (e.g. isSelected) with a visible focus/selected border using color.border.strong or color.action.primary.default, never via internal-only state.
Ensure the card brand icon has an accessible name (e.g. aria-label="Visa") since it conveys meaning, not just decoration.
Define layout behavior for each breakpoint.* token so the merchant dashboard grid does not silently overflow or truncate the masked card number on narrow viewports.
Add an explicit 'selected' visual token/spec (e.g., border-color: var(--color-action-primary-default) at >=2px plus a checkmark icon) so selection is never conveyed by color alone

Model the card group as role="radiogroup" of role="radio" cards (or role="listbox"/aria-selected items) with aria-checked/aria-selected bound to the `selected` state prop, and support Space/Enter to select
Give the masked number an aria-label (e.g., 'Card ending in 1234') separate from its visible masked text, and expose the brand icon's accessible name (e.g., aria-label="Visa") rather than aria-hidden

Make the delete control a distinct, independently focusable icon-button with aria-label="Delete card ending in {last4}", require a confirmation dialog (role="alertdialog") before removal, and announce success/failure via role="status"
Apply var(--color-focus-ring) as a visible focus-visible outline on both the selectable card and the delete button
Wrap any selection or removal animation in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` to disable/shorten it, and show a Spinner with role="status" while a delete request is pending

issues_found: Delete-confirmation dialog (role="alertdialog", isDeleteConfirmOpen) is rendered as a sibling of the radio card, not nested inside it, and the only Escape-key handler (handleKeyDown) is attached to the role="radio" div. Once focus moves into the Cancel/Confirm buttons inside the dialog, pressing Escape never reaches handleKeyDown, so 'Escape cancels and returns focus to the delete Button' (spec.delete_flow, spec.accessibility) is not actually reachable via keyboard.

Spec states loading state a11y: 'delete Button is disabled while loading', but the loading branch (state==='loading') returns early and renders no Button/.actions at all — the disabled delete control does not exist in the DOM, so this spec requirement is unimplemented and untestable as written.

The entire delete_flow (isDeleteRequest -> isDeleteConfirmOpen alertdialog -> Confirm/Cancel -> isDeleting Spinner) has zero test coverage in PaymentCard.test.tsx and zero story coverage in PaymentCard.stories.tsx — none of Confirm, Cancel, Escape-cancel, focus-return-to-delete-button, or the isDeleting Spinner/disabled-controls behavior is exercised anywhere.

Component never composes the 'Card' primitive from @acme/ui despite spec.composes listing it — the card, confirm surface, and all containers are raw <div>s. Not a hallucinated import, but a spec-declared composition that is silently skipped.

axe accessibility smoke test only renders the 'default' state; 'selected', 'loading', 'empty', 'error', and the delete-confirmation surface are never axe-checked, so violations specific to those states (e.g. the alertdialog's missing aria-modal, the error-state radio still carrying role="radio"/aria-checked while non-interactive) would not be caught.
fix_list: Move the Escape-to-cancel keydown handling onto the confirmation overlay/dialog itself (e.g. onKeyDown on the .overlay or .confirmSurface div, or a document-level listener scoped to isDeleteConfirmOpen) so Escape works regardless of which control inside the dialog currently has focus.

Render the delete Button (disabled) in the loading-state branch instead of omitting the .actions/Button entirely, per spec.states.loading a11y text, and add a test asserting the delete Button is present and disabled while state==='loading'.

Add test(s) exercising the delete-confirmation flow: opening isDeleteConfirmOpen renders role="alertdialog" with the expected accessible name, Confirm calls onDeleteConfirm, Cancel calls onDeleteCancel, Escape calls onDeleteCancel and returns focus to the delete Button, and isDeleting renders the Spinner (role="status", aria-label="Deleting card") with Confirm/Cancel disabled.

Add a Storybook story for the delete-confirmation surface (isDeleteConfirmOpen: true, and a second variant with isDeleting: true) so it is covered as a visual-regression fixture per the project's testing conventions.

Either compose the @acme/ui Card primitive for the card/confirm-surface containers or explicitly document in generated.meta.json why Card was intentionally not used, so the spec.composes contract is not silently ignored.

Extend the axe smoke test (or add additional axe assertions) to cover at least the 'error' state and the open delete-confirmation dialog, since those introduce distinct roles (alert, alertdialog) not present in the 'default' state check.
